title: Show review state in PR listing
description: Surface the current review state for each PR in the menu bar listing. States to display: eligible for merge, needs review, needs updating (and potentially others from GitHub's review state API). No configuration needed — just additional info shown per-PR row. This is the highest-value part of surfacing review state, and should be implemented first/independently.

title: Add arbitrary PRs by URL or number to the monitor list
description: Allow users to add any PR to MonitorLizard by URL or repo+number, not just PRs authored by them. This enables use cases like monitoring a teammate's PR while they're out of office. The PR should appear in the main list and be watchable like any other PR.

title: Post a comment to a PR from MonitorLizard
description: Add a 'Comment' action to each PR row. When clicked, show an alert/sheet with a text field where the user can type a comment (e.g. 'doxbot qaready') and submit it. On submit, use 'gh pr comment <number> --repo <owner>/<repo> --body <text>' to post the comment.

title: Allow user to set custom labels for each PR
description:
## Problem
Users want to tag or annotate PRs with their own labels (e.g. 'urgent', 'needs-review', 'blocked') independently of the GitHub labels already on the PR. Useful for personal tracking without pushing anything to GitHub.

## Proposed Solution
Add a custom labels feature allowing users to attach one or more short text labels to any PR. Labels are stored locally and displayed alongside GitHub labels in the PR row.

## Acceptance Criteria
- User can add a custom label to any PR (authored, reviewing, or other)
- User can remove a custom label from a PR
- Custom labels persist across app restarts
- Custom labels are visually distinct from GitHub labels in PRRowView
- Custom labels are keyed by PR id (owner/repo#number)

## Out of Scope
- Syncing custom labels to GitHub
- Sharing custom labels across machines

title: Notify when new comments or approvals arrive on watched PRs
description: Fire a notification (and optionally a voice announcement) when a new comment or approval comes in on a watched PR. Primary use case: author is waiting for approval and wants to know immediately when someone adds comments so they can iterate quickly. Initial design: piggyback off the existing watch toggle so watched PRs also trigger review-activity notifications. This avoids needing new per-PR UI, though a global opt-in setting may be needed for users who watch builds but don't want review notifications.

title: Update PR branch from target branch
description: Add an 'Update Branch' action to each PR row that merges or rebases the PR branch with its target branch. Implement via 'gh pr update-branch <number> --repo <owner>/<repo>'. Should show a confirmation or status indicator while in progress.

title: Consolidate PR row actions into a unified actions UI
description: As more actions are added to PR rows (Watch, Open, Comment, Update Branch), the UI will get cluttered. Explore and implement a cleaner way to surface these actions. Options: (1) A popover/context menu triggered by an ellipsis or chevron button, (2) An expand/collapse toggle to reveal actions inline, (3) Vertical stacking of action buttons on the right side of each row. Goal is to keep the default row compact while making all actions accessible.

title: Integrate Sparkle 2 for auto-updates
description:
## Summary

Add auto-update support to MonitorLizard using Sparkle 2, so the app can check for updates once daily and install them automatically.

## Background

MonitorLizard is distributed outside the Mac App Store, so there's no built-in update mechanism. Sparkle 2 is the standard solution for macOS app auto-updates.

## Implementation Plan

### 1. Add Sparkle 2 via SPM

Add the Sparkle package dependency to the Xcode project.

### 2. EdDSA Signing Setup

- Use Sparkle's `generate_keys` tool to create an EdDSA key pair
- Private key stored in Keychain (never distributed)
- Public key added to `Info.plist` as `SUPublicEDKey`
- Use `sign_update` to sign each release archive

### 3. Appcast Hosting

- Create an `appcast.xml` file (RSS feed listing versions, download URLs, signatures)
- Host on GitHub Pages from the repo (e.g. `gh-pages` branch)
- Each release updates the appcast with version, download URL, EdDSA signature, and file size

### 4. Create UpdaterService

- Wrap `SPUUpdater` in an `UpdaterService`
- Configure automatic check interval to 86400 seconds (24 hours)
- Use `SPUStandardUpdaterController` — it can present its own update window even for LSUIElement apps

### 5. Add "Check for Updates..." Menu Item

- Add a manual "Check for Updates..." button in the MenuBarView footer area (alongside Settings)
- Triggers `SPUUpdater.checkForUpdates()`

### 6. Release Workflow

Script or document the release process:
1. Bump version in Xcode
2. Archive/build the app
3. Zip the `.app` bundle
4. Run `sign_update MonitorLizard.zip` to get EdDSA signature
5. Create a GitHub Release with the zip attached
6. Update `appcast.xml` with new entry
7. Push the appcast

Consider automating steps 2-6 with a shell script or Makefile target.

## Architecture

```
MonitorLizardApp
├─ MenuBarExtra
│  └─ MenuBarView
│     ├─ Header
│     ├─ PR list
│     └─ Footer
│        ├─ Settings button
│        └─ "Check for Updates..." button  ← new
└─ UpdaterService (wraps SPUUpdater)
   ├─ Automatic check every 24h
   └─ Manual check from menu item
```

## Notes

- Sparkle's auto-install quits and relaunches the app. For a menu bar app this is seamless — the icon briefly disappears and reappears.
- EdDSA signing is Sparkle's own mechanism, separate from Apple code signing.
- No Apple code signing is currently in place, but could be added independently.

title: Investigate 1Password Shell Plugin compatibility with gh auth
description:
A user reported that MonitorLizard fails to run gh commands when 1Password's Shell Plugin is active. The plugin works by sourcing ~/.config/op/plugins.sh (added to .zshrc), which sets GITHUB_TOKEN so gh can authenticate. Because MonitorLizard launches gh via ShellExecutor without sourcing the user's shell profile, it never gets GITHUB_TOKEN and authentication fails.

Telltale symptom: gh auth status shows login via GITHUB_TOKEN that cannot be unset with 'unset GITHUB_TOKEN' — 1Password re-injects it each time.

Workaround (user confirmed): disable 1Password Developer settings and remove the op plugins.sh line from .zshrc.

Things to explore:
- Source ~/.config/op/plugins.sh in ShellExecutor's environment setup if it exists
- Or detect the GITHUB_TOKEN pattern and surface a helpful error/onboarding message
- Or document the workaround prominently in README/onboarding

title: Add expandable details section to PR rows (branch name, comment count, etc.)
notes: Luke's feedback: branch name has limited value since it requires mental mapping to a person/context, usually gets truncated anyway, and the PR title already conveys what the branch name would. Suggestion: replace always-visible branch name with a collapsible Details section showing full branch name (untruncated), comment count, and other GitHub metadata (review count, mergeable status, etc). Keeps default row compact while making deeper info accessible on demand.
